no_changes_rationale:
Bead: ddx-18b15451 — artifacts: wire server-side search in frontend, debounce + stale-response drop

OUTCOME: no source changes. All four AC are satisfied by code already merged on main
at base-rev a9726cd3 (this worktree's base). The prior BLOCK was on an attempt whose
diff only contained execution evidence; this rationale points at the evidence in the
already-landed commits so a re-review can resolve the BLOCK.

Primary commit:
  8d1edfa4 fix(web): drop stale artifacts loadMore responses on search race [ddx-18b15451]
  Files:
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/searchFetcher.ts
    cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/searchFetcher.test.ts

Supporting prior commit (added the `search:` arg to the GraphQL query and wired it
into the SSR load() so search is server-side from page 1, not just past page 1):
  13896420 fix(artifacts): mediaType wildcard + server-side search + sort/staleness args [ddx-0ac5f35d]

AC mapping (all satisfied):

1. Frontend search calls server.
   - +page.ts:7-27 — ARTIFACTS_QUERY declares `$search: String` and forwards it to
     `artifacts(search: $search, ...)`.
   - +page.ts:84-117 — load() reads `q` from the URL and passes it as `search` to the
     server on initial render and on every navigation.
   - +page.svelte:67-78,87 — onSearchInput debounces then calls navigateWith({ q }),
     which goto()s the new URL, re-running load() against the server.
   - +page.svelte:168-192 — loadMore() also passes `search: q ? q : undefined` so
     subsequent pages stay filtered server-side. Backend resolver:
     cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go (search applied server-side).
   - The previous client-side `filtered` derivation has been replaced with
     `$derived(allEdges)` (+page.svelte:53-55) with a comment stating the rationale.

2. Debounced (200ms typical).
   - +page.svelte:64,80-89 — clearTimeout/setTimeout(..., 200) on each keystroke
     before navigateWith fires. Matches the AC's "200ms typical" target.

3. Stale responses dropped on race.
   - searchFetcher.ts:11-54 — createRequestSequence() issues monotonic tokens;
     runLatest() returns `{ stale: true }` if a newer call superseded the in-flight
     one; invalidate() drops all in-flight tokens (used when search query changes).
   - +page.svelte:65,80-89,168-192 — fetchSeq is invalidated on each search input,
     and loadMore() runs through runLatest(fetchSeq, ...). Stale results are
     short-circuited (`if (outcome.stale) return`) so they cannot be appended on
     top of a newer page-1.

4. Tests cover correctness past page 1.
   - searchFetcher.test.ts (8 tests, all passing — verified locally with
     `bunx vitest run searchFetcher` in cli/internal/server/frontend):
       * "marks only the latest token as current"
       * "invalidate() drops all in-flight tokens"
       * "returns false for a never-issued token"
       * "returns the value when no newer call superseded it"
       * "drops the older response when a newer call resolves first" — directly
         simulates the artifacts search race (user types "fo" then "foo"; the
         "fo" page-1 response arrives after "foo" page-1 has painted)
       * "drops a response invalidated mid-flight (e.g. search changed)"
       * "treats successive paginated calls (page 1, page 2) as non-stale when
         uncontended" — explicit page-1/page-2 correctness assertion
       * "swallows errors from stale responses but propagates fresh errors"
   - e2e/artifacts.spec.ts mocks /graphql to honor the `search` variable on
     the server side, so the search input is exercised against the server
     contract end-to-end.

Local verification performed in this worktree:
  $ cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx vitest run searchFetcher
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  8 passed (8)

Conclusion: No new source change is needed. Reviewer should re-review against
the merged state on main, where commits 8d1edfa4 and 13896420 collectively
satisfy all four AC.
